verdict.answer_first: Tie: both open with the real answer, A: "It's an infinite loop", B: "It's an infinite loop."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly: "The integer-counter version is better"; B surveys more: "Fixes, roughly in order of preference" plus three options.
verdict.fabrication: Both add shaky specifics: A says "until x overflows to Infinity"; B says "around 2^53" and both claim the `<` version "runs 10 times" though it can run 11 here.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the key fix and names the preferred one without extra detours.
